compiled_state.title: Implement merge conflict detection in per-worktree-review-commit.md
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Update plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/per-worktree-review-commit.md to handle merge conflicts:

1. After git merge attempt: check exit code
2. If merge fails (exit != 0):
   a. Run git merge --abort to clean up
   b. Create ticket comment: .claude/scripts/dso ticket comment <story-id> "CONFLICT: worktree <worktree-name> blocked"
   c. Add worktree to conflict queue (do NOT remove worktree)
   d. Continue with next worktree (non-conflicting ones proceed)
3. After all non-conflicting worktrees merged:
   a. For each conflicting worktree: queue for re-implementation against post-merge session state
   b. Re-implementation is serialized (one at a time, each against latest session state)

### Files to modify
- plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/per-worktree-review-commit.md (EDIT)

### TDD Requirement
RED tests from T1 must turn GREEN.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests pass
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-worktree-merge-conflict.sh
- [ ] Prompt contains conflict detection
  Verify: grep -qi 'CONFLICT.*worktree\|merge.*abort\|conflict.*queue' plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/per-worktree-review-commit.md
- [ ] Non-conflicting worktrees continue
  Verify: grep -qi 'continue.*next\|non.conflicting' plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/per-worktree-review-commit.md

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 1+2: Read ticket e12e-aacc and both files. Current per-worktree-review-commit.md Step 6 has brief conflict handling (just 'Run git merge --abort. Flag for re-implementation. DO NOT remove worktree. Continue to next worktree.'). Tests verify: (1) merge abort succeeds and worktree retained, (2) non-conflicting worktrees continue after conflict abort, (3) conflicting worktree stays queued while successful ones are cleaned up. Need to expand Step 6 with: explicit conflict queue, ticket comment command, serialized re-implementation protocol.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 3+4: Implementation complete. Expanded Step 6 in per-worktree-review-commit.md with: explicit conflict detection (exit != 0), git merge --abort, ticket comment command, conflict queue with DO NOT remove worktree, continue processing next worktree. Added 'Conflict queue - re-implementation protocol' section: serialized re-implementation against latest session state, full Steps 2-7 flow for each, escalate to user on second conflict. Acceptance criteria: both greps PASS. Tests: 21/21 PASS (0 failures).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fixed: Conflict handling added to per-worktree-review-commit.md. 21 tests pass.
comments.author: Test
